desc: Enemies are spawned without excluding the player's rectangle, so initialization or respawning can award points immediately and repeatedly without a new player collision.
Movement uses a fixed number of pixels per frame instead of elapsed time, causing player speed to decrease whenever the game cannot sustain 27 FPS.
The elif-based input handling gives left and up priority when opposite direction keys are held, rather than canceling the opposing inputs.
drawEverything constructs a new system Font on every frame, causing avoidable repeated resource allocation in the hot rendering loop.
initGame appends enemies and does not reset player, score, running, or enemy state, so initializing another game in the same process produces stale and duplicate state.
Core game state is spread across mutable module globals, tightly coupling functions and making lifecycle management, testing, and multiple game instances error-prone.
